Welty, E. (1941). A Worn Path. In The Collected Stories of Eudora Welty (pp. 105-111). Houghton Mifflin Harcourt.



Attached File: week 5.docx
from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 8, 2025 6:26PM
Kofi Carr
Mr Wu

I really enjoyed reading your analysis of the symbolism in Welty’s “A Worn Path.” You did a great job connecting Phoenix Jackson’s name to the mythical phoenix, highlighting how it symbolizes rebirth and resilience. I’d love to build on your interpretation by exploring how Phoenix’s name also ties into broader themes of identity and survival in the story.


Phoenix Jackson is not just a character who faces hardship; she’s a representation of the continuity of African American culture and history, especially in the context of the South. Her name evokes the African American experience, a community that has risen from historical struggles, like slavery and segregation, much like the phoenix rising from the ashes. In this way, Phoenix’s name holds both personal and collective significance. It speaks to the resilience not just of an individual, but of a whole community.
